hints.to be ignorant: That would be 'ignorare' — 'Agnosco' comes from 'agnoscere' (to recognize)
hints.to know: That would be 'scire' or 'cognoscere' — 'agnoscere' specifically means 'to recognize'
hints.to learn: That would be 'discere' — 'Agnosco' comes from 'agnoscere' (to recognize)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'agnoscam' — 'Agnosco' with -o is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'agnovi' — 'Agnosco' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'agnoscam' — 'Agnosco' with -o is present indicative

hints.nosco, noscere, novi, notum: These are the parts of 'noscere' (to learn/come to know) — the simple verb, not the compound
hints.cognosco, cognoscere, cognovi, cognitum: These are the parts of 'cognoscere' (to learn/know) — different compound prefix
hints.ignosco, ignoscere, ignovi, ignotum: These are the parts of 'ignoscere' (to forgive/pardon)

steps.reveal: veteris = of the old (gen. sg., modifying 'flammae')

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'vetus' — 'veteris' with -is is genitive singular (3rd decl.)
hints.accusative: Accusative singular would be 'veterem' — 'veteris' is genitive
hints.ablative: Ablative singular would be 'vetere' — 'veteris' with -is is genitive

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'veteris' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'veteris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'veteris' ends in -is

hints.nominative: While 'vestigia' could be nom. pl. neuter, here it is accusative, object of 'agnosco'
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'vestigiis' — 'vestigia' is accusative plural neuter
hints.vocative: Vocative plural neuter would look the same, but 'vestigia' is accusative here

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'vestigii' ends in -ii (2nd declension neuter)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'vestigii' ends in -ii
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'vestigii' ends in -ii

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'flammae' too, but here it is genitive singular modifying 'vestigia'
hints.dative: Dative singular also looks like 'flammae', but context shows genitive — traces 'of' the flame
hints.vocative: Vocative would be 'flamma' — 'flammae' is genitive singular here

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'flammae' ends in -ae (1st declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'flammae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'flammae' ends in -ae

translation: I recognize the traces of the old flame
